input.description: Check chromium and saml2aws in workspace image

content.text:
This is a **workspace image / devcontainer lifecycle** failure, not a bug in the Agno provisioner’s `devpod up` wrapper. The provisioner did its job: it ran `devpod up` with your pinned image and surfaced the failure correctly.

## What failed

`devpod up` got through image pull, container setup, and workspace chown, then died in the **image’s built-in devcontainer post-create hook** (the long `bash -c 'set -euo pipefail; BAKE=…'` command in the log). Agno’s later SSH bootstrap (`ProvisionerBootstrapService`) never runs if this step fails.

Your `.env` points at:

`REBASE_DEVPOD_WORKSPACE_IMAGE=public.ecr.aws/k1t8c0v2/devpod-generic@sha256:39e00571ccf52f1d3e90be2e8892821071d17a7a99226688d51fd349957537c6`

## Root cause (verified on that digest)

The image **does** set `DEVCONTAINER_BAKE_ROOT=/opt/devpod-image` and has the bake tree (`post-create.sh`, `package.json`, etc.). The lifecycle copy step is fine.

`post-create.sh` fails immediately on:

```bash
test -x /usr/bin/chromium
```

On this image:

- `saml2aws` is present (`/usr/local/bin/saml2aws`)
- **`/usr/bin/chromium` does not exist**

So the hook exits with status 1. DevPod only reports “lifecycle hooks: failed … exit status 1”, which is why the log looks like a generic bake/`DEVCONTAINER_BAKE_ROOT` problem even though the real issue is missing Chromium.

The Docker daemon `containerd-snapshotter` warnings in stderr are unrelated.

## What to do

1. **Read the full transcript** on the provision host:  
   `/home/ubuntu/workdir/provisioner/rewrite-me-fc56fb/devpod-up.log`  
   (or SSH to `34.207.213.21` and inspect that path). You should see the post-create trace stopping at the Chromium check.

2. **Fix the workspace image** (in whatever repo builds `devpod-generic`, using `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.embed`):
   - Install Chromium (or whatever path `post-create.sh` expects), **or**
   - Change `post-create.sh` / `CHROMIUM_BIN` to match what the image actually ships.

3. **Publish a new digest** and update `REBASE_DEVPOD_WORKSPACE_IMAGE` in `.env` / compose.

Quick smoke test after rebuild:

```bash
docker run --rm --user node public.ecr.aws/k1t8c0v2/devpod-generic@sha256:<new-digest> \
  bash -c 'test -x /usr/bin/chromium || test -x "${CHROMIUM_BIN:-/usr/bin/chromium}"'
```

## Agno-side note

`DevpodWorkspaceService` only runs image-based `devpod up` + SSH verify; it does not control the image’s devcontainer hook. No change in this repo will fix a broken `devpod-generic` post-create unless you change the image reference or build pipeline.

If you want, I can help trace where `devpod-generic` / `Dockerfile.embed` is built (likely outside this repo) or tighten provisioner failure hints to call out “post-create / Chromium” when the transcript matches this pattern.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
The problem is a **contract mismatch inside [trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image](https://github.com/trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image)** — not missing `DEVCONTAINER_BAKE_ROOT` and not the Agno provisioner.

## What breaks on `devpod up`

DevPod runs `postCreateCommand` from `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`. That copies the bake tree from `/opt/devpod-image` and runs `post-create.sh`. The published digest you use (`sha256:39e00571…`) fails on the **first hard check** in that script:

```bash
CHROMIUM_BIN="${CHROMIUM_BIN:-/usr/bin/chromium}"
command -v saml2aws >/dev/null
test -x "$CHROMIUM_BIN"   # ← exits 1: no such file
```

On that image, `saml2aws` is present (`/usr/local/bin/saml2aws` from `Dockerfile.embed`), but **`/usr/bin/chromium` is not installed anywhere**. DevPod only reports “lifecycle hooks … exit status 1”, which is easy to misread as a bake-root problem.

## Why the repo is inconsistent

Three layers disagree:

| Piece | Says |
|--------|------|
| `devcontainer.json` | `CHROME_BIN: "/usr/bin/chromium"` |
| `post-create.sh` | Assumes Chromium is “baked” and checks it **before** Playwright setup |
| `Dockerfile.embed` | Installs saml2aws, devpod, gsd-pi, tmux, ttyd, ngrok — **no Chromium** |
| `scripts/ensure-playwright-browsers.sh` | Installs Playwright’s Chromium under `~/.cache/ms-playwright` via `npx playwright install chromium` |

Build is two-stage (`build-image.sh`):

1. **Stage 1** — `devcontainer build` from `devcontainer.json` (Node 22 + GitHub/AWS CLI + docker-in-docker features).
2. **Stage 2** — `Dockerfile.embed` bakes scripts into `/opt/devpod-image`.

Stage 1 does not add a system Chromium package. Stage 2’s comment claims Chromium is baked, but the Dockerfile never installs it. Playwright’s browser is only installed **later** in `ensure-playwright-browsers.sh`, which `post-create.sh` calls **after** the `test -x /usr/bin/chromium` line.

So the image published by CI (including the recent “ngrok, ttyd install” run) is internally inconsistent: **post-create requires a binary the build never produces.**

## What is actually fine

- `DEVCONTAINER_BAKE_ROOT=/opt/devpod-image` is set correctly in the published image.
- Bake copy (`package.json`, `post-create.sh`, `scripts/`) works.
- Recent embed additions (tmux, ttyd, ngrok) are unrelated to this failure.

## Fixes (in `generic-devpod-image`)

Pick one coherent approach:

1. **Install system Chromium in the image** (stage 1 feature or `apt install chromium` in `Dockerfile.embed`) so `/usr/bin/chromium` exists before `post-create.sh` runs — matches current `CHROME_BIN` and the early `test -x`.

2. **Reorder / relax post-create** — run `ensure-playwright-browsers.sh` first, then set `CHROMIUM_BIN` to Playwright’s binary (or drop the early `test -x` and only assert after Playwright install).

3. **Align env** — stop advertising `CHROME_BIN=/usr/bin/chromium` until something actually installs it there.

After fix: rebuild, push a new digest to `public.ecr.aws/k1t8c0v2/devpod-generic`, update `REBASE_DEVPOD_WORKSPACE_IMAGE` in `.env`.

Smoke test:

```bash
docker run --rm public.ecr.aws/k1t8c0v2/devpod-generic@sha256:<new> \
  bash -c 'test -x /usr/bin/chromium || (cd /tmp && cp -a /opt/devpod-image/. . && bash .devcontainer/post-create.sh)'
```

Until that lands, any consumer (including Agno’s provisioner) will keep failing at the same devcontainer lifecycle step with the digest you have pinned today.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
## Verdict

**The older ECR image is fine.** Your current digest is broken by **today’s** `Dockerfile.embed` change.

| Image | Digest (short) | Created | `/usr/bin/chromium` | `post-create` chromium check |
|--------|----------------|---------|---------------------|------------------------------|
| [Older (AWS console)](https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/ecr/repositories/public/346945241475/devpod-generic/_/image/sha256:6a4ce8c99d8f522304897b48ba4a59356cb7e9cb98058d4e0d8dcf6d9bf39694/details?region=us-east-1) | `6a4ce8c9…` | **2026-05-11** | Present (`chromium` deb, executable) | Passes |
| Current in `.env` | `39e00571…` | **2026-05-31** | Missing | Fails at `test -x /usr/bin/chromium` |

So the provisioner failure is **not** a long-standing bake-root bug; it started with the image published after today’s ngrok/ttyd work.

## What broke it today

Only one file changed in the breaking publish: `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.embed` in commit [`4a27b1c`](https://github.com/trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image/commit/4a27b1c) — **“ngrok, ttyd install”** (CI run at 06:59 UTC).

**Before (tmux commit `212d320`, still OK):**
```dockerfile
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends chromium tmux
```

**After (`4a27b1c`, broken):**
```dockerfile
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
     ca-certificates curl openssl tmux \
```

`chromium` was dropped when the apt line was refactored to add ngrok/ttyd/curl deps. `post-create.sh` and `CHROME_BIN=/usr/bin/chromium` were unchanged, so lifecycle still requires a binary that the new image no longer installs.

The earlier **“install tmux”** commit (`212d320`) still installed `chromium`; that did **not** cause the regression.

## Timeline

- **May 11** — Image matching your old digest (still has Chromium) — aligns with May 5 `Update Dockerfile.embed` that installed `chromium`.
- **May 31 ~05:31** — tmux added; chromium still in Dockerfile.
- **May 31 ~06:59** — ngrok/ttyd commit; chromium removed → digest `39e00571…` fails `devpod up`.

## What to do

1. **Immediate workaround:** Pin back to the known-good digest:
   ```bash
   REBASE_DEVPOD_WORKSPACE_IMAGE=public.ecr.aws/k1t8c0v2/devpod-generic@sha256:6a4ce8c99d8f522304897b48ba4a59356cb7e9cb98058d4e0d8dcf6d9bf39694
   ```
   (You lose today’s ttyd/ngrok layers until a fixed image is published.)

2. **Fix in [generic-devpod-image](https://github.com/trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image):** Add `chromium` back to the first apt `RUN` in `Dockerfile.embed`, e.g.:
   ```dockerfile
   ca-certificates curl openssl tmux chromium \
   ```
   Rebuild, push, update `REBASE_DEVPOD_WORKSPACE_IMAGE` to the new digest.

**Note:** The older image still runs Cursor Agent install on first post-create (network-dependent); that’s separate from the Chromium regression.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

</user_query>

  && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \

input.command:
cd /workspaces/generic-devpod-image && git add .devcontainer/Dockerfile.embed && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(embed): restore chromium dropped in ngrok/ttyd apt refactor

post-create.sh requires /usr/bin/chromium; commit 4a27b1c removed it when
splitting apt installs. Fail embed build if chromium is missing.
EOF
)" && git push origin main && git status

input.command: sleep 5 && gh run list -R trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image --limit 3
input.description: Check CI publish workflow started after push

content.text:
Done. There wasn’t a local clone, so I cloned [trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image](https://github.com/trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image), applied the fix, and pushed to `main`.

## Change

In `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.embed`:

- Put **`chromium`** back in the apt install list (it was dropped in `4a27b1c` when ngrok/ttyd were added).
- Added **`test -x /usr/bin/chromium`** next to the `tmux` / `ttyd` / `ngrok` check so a bad embed build fails in CI instead of at `devpod up`.

## Commit

`3617d88` — `fix(embed): restore chromium dropped in ngrok/ttyd apt refactor`  
https://github.com/trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image/commit/3617d88

## CI

**Publish to ECR Public** is running for that push (~5 min):  
https://github.com/trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image/actions/runs/26707137589

When it finishes, grab the new digest from [ECR](https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/ecr/repositories/public/346945241475/devpod-generic) and update `REBASE_DEVPOD_WORKSPACE_IMAGE` in agno `.env` (or pin the new `@sha256:…` digest).

Until then you can keep using the known-good digest:

`sha256:6a4ce8c99d8f522304897b48ba4a59356cb7e9cb98058d4e0d8dcf6d9bf39694`

I can watch CI and update agno `.env` with the new digest once publish completes — say if you want that.

[REDACTED]
